title: Thiessen (2011): A Grammar of SignWriting
subtitle: A review of real structural insight placed one level too low
summary: Thiessen recognizes rule-governed SignWriting structure but places the main abstraction one level too low.
synopsis: Thiessen is one of the stronger early engagements with SignWriting. The thesis correctly recognizes rule-governed structure and computational need, but mislocates the main abstraction at the level of symbols rather than at the level of the written sign and sign text.
cover_image_alt: An open manuscript of SignWriting-like symbols with grammar cards, spatial rule diagrams, parsing grids, and a magnifying glass.

slides.title: What Thiessen Sees
slides.summary: Thiessen is one of the stronger early engagements with SignWriting because it takes structure, space, and computation seriously.
slides.speech: This review treats Thiessen as one of the stronger early works. The thesis takes SignWriting seriously as writing, recognizes spatial organization, does real script-internal descriptive work, and sees the computational problem early.
slides.notes: This is a sympathetic review, not a dismissal.

image_prompts.concrete: Review slide crediting Thiessen for taking SignWriting structure, spatial organization, descriptive work, and computation seriously
image_prompts.abstract: An early serious engagement with structure and space brought into focus

slides.title: Where The Analysis Stops Short
slides.summary: The thesis correctly identifies structure but locates too much explanatory weight at the symbol level rather than the written-sign level.
slides.speech: The central critique is that the thesis is one abstraction level too low. It correctly identifies structure, but the main explanatory weight sits too heavily at the symbol level rather than at the level of written signs and sign text.
slides.notes: This is the title claim of the review.

image_prompts.concrete: Critical review slide showing analysis centered on symbols while the written sign and sign text sit one abstraction level higher
image_prompts.abstract: A ladder of abstraction from symbols up to written signs and sign text

slides.title: Framework Reading
slides.summary: Through the framework, Thiessen is strong on spatial organization and symbol analysis, but transitional on structural unit and text model.
slides.speech: Through the framework, Thiessen is strong on spatial organization and symbol analysis. The limits appear in structural unit analysis and text-model development. The work is serious and useful, but transitional.
slides.notes: Use transitional as the key assessment.

image_prompts.concrete: Layer-based reading slide separating writing need, alphabetic bias, structural unit analysis, spatial organization, and claims versus evidence
image_prompts.abstract: A source claim passed through a clear layer-based review frame

slides.title: Durable Contribution
slides.summary: The thesis establishes that SignWriting has describable internal structure, while showing why a higher-level sign-text model is needed.
bullets: The written sign needs to be centered
slides.speech: The durable contribution is substantial. Thiessen helps establish that SignWriting has describable internal structure and a real encoding problem. The review adds that the written sign and sign text need to become the governing abstraction.
slides.notes: Close with value plus next step.

image_prompts.concrete: Contribution slide showing the necessity-of-writing argument preserved while the orthographic prescription is bounded
image_prompts.abstract: A valid contribution kept intact after critique
